description: A book-first MVP architecture providing a calm, editorial learning environment with AI-assisted study tools deployed on Railway.

features.title: Three-Service Architecture
descriptions: The OpenClaw template implements a clean three-service architecture designed for Railway deployment: a public-facing Next.js web application, an internal core service running OpenClaw/QMD/SFTPGo, and an internal MongoDB database.

This architecture enforces a strict security boundary where only the web service is publicly exposed. All browser traffic flows through Next.js, which then makes authenticated internal calls to the core service and MongoDB.
The design supports Railway's sleep/scale-to-zero patterns while maintaining a responsive user experience through graceful degradation and clear status communication.
howItsUsed: Users interact with the book content through the Next.js web interface at the public URL
The web service handles all authentication, session management, and serves the Book-first UI
When AI assistance is needed, the web service makes internal API calls to the core service
Search functionality routes through the web service to QMD (Query Markup Database) in the core container
File uploads and administrative tasks can optionally use the SFTPGo component within the core service
All persistent data is stored in MongoDB, accessed only by the web service (and optionally core)
The architecture allows the core service to sleep during periods of inactivity while the web remains responsive

howToConfigure.action: Create Railway services from the monorepo
howToConfigure.details: Set up four services: 'web' (services/web), 'core' (services/core), 'mongo' (services/mongo/nodes), and optionally 'sftpgo' (services/sftpgo)
Enable public network only on the 'web' service. Keep 'core', 'mongo', and 'sftpgo' internal-only

howToConfigure.action: Set environment variables for web service
howToConfigure.details: Configure MONGODB_URI, INTERNAL_CORE_BASE_URL, INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN or INTERNAL_JWT_SIGNING_KEYS, AUTH_SECRET, and AUTH_URL

howToConfigure.action: Set environment variables for core service
howToConfigure.details: Configure INTERNAL_SERVICE_TOKEN (must match web), SETUP_PASSWORD, OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR, OPENCLAW_WORKSPACE_DIR, OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN
Configure REPLICA_SET_NAME, MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME, MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD, KEYFILE
Add /data volume to core service for persistence. Optional /data volume for sftpgo if used
Ensure services can communicate via Railway internal DNS: web.railway.internal, core.railway.internal, mongo.railway.internal

features.title: Book-First UI System
descriptions: The Book-first UI implements a 'Calm Research UI' pattern prioritizing reading, retention, and recall over trading execution. It features a stable three-column sacred shell layout that remains consistent across all book-related pages.
The design philosophy emphasizes editorial-first typography, calm density, quiet chrome with minimal borders, restrained color usage, and no empty pages. Every state has purpose and a next action.

The UI includes six required pages: Library (book index), Reader (section view), Notes (filterable collection), Playbooks (draft to published lifecycle), Admin (status and reindex), and Login (secure authentication).
howItsUsed: Readers navigate through the book using the Left Rail TOC Tree showing Parts, Chapters, and Sections with progress indicators
The Center Content area displays section content with structured blocks: TL;DR (up to 3 bullets), Checklist (up to 5 items), Common Mistakes (up to 3), and Drill (1 exercise)
The Right Rail Agent Panel provides AI-assisted study tools including Explain/Rephrase, Socratic Tutor, Flashcards/Quiz, Checklist Builder, Scenario Tree Builder, and Notes Assistant
Users create highlights by selecting text in the Reader, which triggers a floating toolbar with Highlight, Add Note, and Copy options
The Command Palette (Cmd+K) provides quick access to search, navigation, note creation, and admin functions
Responsive adaptation provides full 3-column layout on large screens, 2-column with drawers on medium screens, and mobile-optimized sheets on small screens
Notes and highlights are linked to specific sections and anchors, creating a personal knowledge graph

howToConfigure.details: Set BOOK_SOURCE_MODE (manifest or directory), BOOK_SOURCE_DIR, BOOK_IMPORT_MANIFEST, BOOK_CANONICAL_COLLECTION, BOOK_TOC_COLLECTION
Set BOOK_IMPORT_ENABLED to true/false and BOOK_IMPORT_DRY_RUN to test imports without writing to database
Modify components in services/web/src/components/ using shadcn/ui patterns. Key components include TOC tree, Reader blocks, Agent panel, and Command palette
Default breakpoints: ≥1536px (2xl) full 3-column, ≥1280px (xl) 2-column + drawer, ≥1024px (lg) 1-column + drawers, <1024px mobile sheets
Default shortcuts: Cmd+K (Command palette), 'n' (New note in Reader), 'b' (Toggle bookmark), '?' (Shortcut help)
Control which skills appear in the Agent Panel: explain, socratic, flashcards, checklist, scenario_tree, notes_assist

features.title: MongoDB Data Layer
descriptions: The MongoDB schema supports the Book-first MVP with collections for book content (sections and TOC), user-generated content (notes, highlights, bookmarks, reading progress), playbooks (draft to published), agent run history, and audit logging.
The schema uses MongoDB ObjectIds for identifiers, ISO date timestamps, and section slugs as canonical identifiers. It supports draft/published workflows and optional versioning.
Indexes are carefully designed to support the primary read patterns: Library TOC loading, Reader section fetching, per-user notes/highlights, and playbook management.
howItsUsed: Book sections are stored in the book_sections collection with slug-based lookup, hierarchical part/chapter/section structure, markdown body, frontmatter metadata, and heading anchors
The book_toc collection provides a cached TOC tree for fast Library rendering without aggregating sections on every request
User notes in the notes collection link to sections via sectionSlug and optionally anchorId, supporting text selection ranges and tags
Highlights store text ranges with optional color coding and can link to notes via noteId
Bookmarks provide quick save points with unique constraints per user/section/anchor combination
Reading progress tracks completion percentage and last position for the 'Continue reading' feature
Playbooks store draft and published trading procedures with triggers, checklists, scenario trees, and linked sections
Agent runs are logged for audit and replay, storing skill type, context, output, and saved references
The audit_log collection tracks admin actions, indexing operations, config changes, and security events

howToConfigure.details: Set MONGODB_URI in web service (e.g., mongodb://mongo.railway.internal:27017/natealma)
Configure MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME and MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD for MongoDB service
Set BOOK_CANONICAL_COLLECTION (default: book_sections) and BOOK_TOC_COLLECTION (default: book_toc)
Optional: Create text index on bodyMarkdown and titles for MongoDB fallback search when QMD is unavailable
Set up TTL indexes for rate limiting (2 hours) and agent run history retention as needed
Implement scheduled logical dumps or use Railway's managed backup features

features.title: Internal Service Authentication
descriptions: Internal service authentication secures communication between the web service and core service using short-lived signed JWTs. This ensures that even though core is internal-only, it does not rely on network secrecy for security.
The JWT implementation uses HS256 algorithm with dual-key rotation support, allowing seamless key updates without service interruption. Tokens have a short 5-15 minute TTL for security.
All internal endpoints require valid JWTs with proper audience (aud: 'core'), issuer (iss: 'web'), and expiration validation.
howItsUsed: When the web service needs to call core (for search, agent skills, or indexing), it generates a signed JWT with the current active key
The JWT includes claims for issuer, audience, subject, issued-at, expiration, and a unique token ID (jti)
Optional claims include user ID (uid) for per-user rate limiting, role for authorization, and request ID (rid) for tracing
The web service includes the JWT in the Authorization header: 'Bearer <jwt>' along with X-Request-Id for correlation
Core validates the JWT signature, checks expiration, verifies aud/iss claims, and extracts user context for authorization decisions
Admin-only endpoints (like reindex) require role=admin claim in the JWT
Key rotation uses a dual-key strategy: web signs with the new active key while core accepts both old and new keys during transition

howToConfigure.details: Create random secrets for JWT signing. Use cryptographically secure random generation (e.g., openssl rand -base64 32)
Set INTERNAL_JWT_SIGNING_KEYS as JSON array: [{"kid":"k1","secret":"<random>","active":true}]
Set INTERNAL_JWT_VERIFY_KEYS with the same JSON array in core service environment
Configure JWT expiration between 5-15 minutes (default recommended: 10 minutes)
1) Add new key to both services, 2) Deploy core, 3) Deploy web (activates new key), 4) Wait 24-72 hours, 5) Remove old key
Configure web service to log auth failures to audit_log collection with rate limiting to prevent log spam

features.title: Agent Skills & AI Integration
descriptions: The Agent Skills system provides AI-assisted study tools through the right rail Agent Panel. Six core skills help users understand, retain, and apply book content: Explain/Rephrase, Socratic Tutor, Flashcards/Quiz, Checklist Builder, Scenario Tree Builder, and Notes Assistant.
Each skill is context-aware, receiving the current section content, selected text, and user preferences to generate relevant outputs. Skills can save results as notes or playbook drafts.
The system is designed with graceful degradation: when the core service is unavailable, the UI shows clear status messages and allows users to continue reading uninterrupted.
howItsUsed: Explain/Rephrase offers three modes: Simple (plain language explanation), Technical (precise terminology), and Analogy (relatable comparisons)
Socratic Tutor generates 3-5 questions to test understanding of the current section, encouraging active recall
Flashcards/Quiz creates 5-10 Q&A pairs suitable for spaced repetition study
Checklist Builder generates pre/during/post trade checklists based on section content
Scenario Tree Builder creates If/Then decision trees that can be saved as playbook drafts
Notes Assistant helps create, tag, and link notes to sections with AI-generated suggestions
Users select text in the Reader and invoke skills via the Agent Panel or keyboard shortcuts
Skill outputs include Copy, Save as Note, and Save as Playbook Draft actions
Agent runs are logged to the agent_runs collection for audit and replay capabilities

howToConfigure.details: Ensure core service exposes POST /internal/agent/run endpoint with proper authentication
Configure available skills in web service: explain, socratic, flashcards, checklist, scenario_tree, notes_assist
Set agent skill limits: 10 per minute per user, 100 per hour per user (adjustable in rate limiting config)
Configure response formats for each skill type: text, qa, cards, checklist, tree
Configure which skills suggest saving to notes vs playbook drafts based on output type
Set up MongoDB text search as fallback when QMD semantic search is unavailable

features.title: Security & Rate Limiting
descriptions: The security layer implements defense in depth with authentication, rate limiting, and audit logging. Rate limits prevent abuse and accidental loops while preserving the calm UX with clear, non-accusatory error messages.
MongoDB-backed counters with TTL ensure rate limits survive process restarts and work under Railway's sleep/scale-to-zero constraints.
All security events are logged with correlation IDs for traceability and debugging.
howItsUsed: Login endpoints enforce 5 attempts per 15 minutes per IP with lockout messaging
Agent skills are limited to 10 per minute and 100 per hour per user to prevent API abuse
Search endpoints allow 30 requests per minute per user with caching for repeated queries
Admin actions (reindex, publish, import) are throttled to 5 per minute per admin user
Rate limit responses include 429 status, error code, human-readable message, and Retry-After header
Failed authentication attempts are logged to the audit_log collection with IP and timestamp
CORS is locked down to the web domain only, preventing cross-origin attacks
Service-to-service authentication uses short-lived JWTs with mandatory validation

howToConfigure.details: Create rate_limits collection in MongoDB with fields: key, windowStart, count, createdAt
Create TTL index on createdAt field with 2-hour expiration for automatic cleanup
Configure key patterns: login:ip:{ip}, agent:user:{userId}, search:user:{userId}, admin:user:{userId}
Modify default limits in code or environment: login (5/15min), agent (10/min, 100/hr), search (30/min), admin (5/min)
Set allowed origins to web domain only in web service configuration
Configure audit_log collection and ensure all admin actions, auth failures, and agent runs are logged

features.title: Sleep/Scale-to-Zero UX
descriptions: The Sleep/Scale-to-Zero UX pattern handles periods when the core service is sleeping or unreachable due to Railway's scale-to-zero behavior. The design ensures no silent failures while maintaining a calm, editorial experience.
When core is unavailable, the UI transitions through 'Waking' and 'Unavailable' states with clear messaging, retry options, and the ability to continue reading uninterrupted.
The pattern includes telemetry logging to help tune sleep timeouts and detect recurring availability issues.
howItsUsed: The web service detects core unavailability via request timeouts (5-8 seconds), 502/503 errors, or failed health checks
Agent Panel shows 'Waking' state with message 'Waking assistant... This can take a few seconds after inactivity' and a Retry button
If core doesn't respond after timeout, the UI shows 'Unavailable' state with 'Assistant temporarily unavailable. Try again in a moment'
Users can always continue reading book content even when the agent is unavailable
Search falls back to MongoDB text search when QMD is unavailable, or shows 'Search is waking up' with retry option
Admin reindex shows explicit error 'Core service is asleep/unreachable' with 'Wake and retry' CTA and last run details
Optional auto-retry occurs once after 2 seconds, then requires user-initiated retry
Telemetry logs track core_unavailable events, time-to-first-success after waking, and retry attempts

howToConfigure.details: Configure 5-8 second timeout for core service requests (recommended: 8 seconds)
Set health check polling frequency to detect core availability changes
Configure MongoDB text search as fallback when QMD semantic search is unavailable
Modify waking/unavailable messages in UI components to match your tone
Enable optional auto-retry once after 2 seconds (disabled by default)
Configure logging of core_unavailable events and time-to-first-success metrics

features.title: Operations & Deployment
descriptions: The operations layer provides deterministic workflows for maintaining the OpenClaw deployment via SSH control plane. It follows a strict command lifecycle: Probe, State, Snapshot, Mutate, Verify, Record, Learn.
The core container uses s6-overlay as a process supervisor to manage OpenClaw, QMD, and SFTPGo with proper startup sequencing, health checks, and graceful shutdown.
Traceability is enforced through correlation IDs, severity markers (INFO, WARN, ERROR, RISK), and continuity checkpoints that survive session interruptions.
howItsUsed: Operators use the openclaw CLI for deployment tasks: openclaw --version, openclaw status, openclaw doctor
The startup sequence initializes configs, validates environment variables, starts QMD (waits for health), starts OpenClaw (waits for health), then starts SFTPGo
Health endpoints provide aggregate status: GET /internal/health returns { ok, components: { qmd, agent, sftpgo } }
Reindex operations are jobs with startedAt, state, progress, and error tracking. They are idempotent and safe to retry
Gateway lock handling probes existing gateways before restart to avoid unnecessary service interruption
Validation gates ensure openclaw --version, openclaw status, openclaw doctor, and /setup/api/status all pass before considering deployment complete
Audit logs track all admin actions with trace IDs for failure traceability
Continuity checkpoints at ../srv/state/continuity.json preserve session state across interruptions

howToConfigure.details: Set ports for QMD (default: 7100), OpenClaw (default: 7200), SFTPGo (default: 7300), optional aggregate health (default: 7000)
Configure process supervisor in core Dockerfile with restart policies per process
Set QMD health timeout (default: 60 seconds). Mark degraded but continue booting if exceeded
Configure log root at ../srv/logs with proper permissions
Set continuity checkpoint path at ../srv/state/continuity.json with required fields: traceId, tool, phase, status, details, updatedAtUtc
Ensure /setup/api/status returns configured:true and /setup/api/debug returns healthy diagnostics
Set restartPolicyType to ON_FAILURE with maxRetries: 10 in railway.json

web.name: INTERNAL_JWT_SIGNING_KEYS
web.description: JSON array of JWT signing keys

core.name: INTERNAL_JWT_VERIFY_KEYS
core.description: JSON array of JWT verification keys
